templates.title: System Security Plan (SSP)
templates.description: Core authorization document describing the system, its security controls, and implementation details. Required for all FedRAMP authorizations.

sections.name: System Information
sections.description: System name, description, categorization, and boundary definition.

sections.name: System Environment
sections.description: Hardware, software, network, and cloud infrastructure details.

sections.name: Security Controls Implementation
sections.description: Detailed implementation statements for all applicable NIST 800-53 controls.

sections.name: Roles and Responsibilities
sections.description: Security roles, responsibilities, and personnel requirements.

requiredFields.name: System Name
requiredFields.description: Official name of the information system
requiredFields.azureSource: Azure Resource Manager — resource group names and tags

requiredFields.name: FIPS 199 Category
requiredFields.example: Moderate (Confidentiality: Moderate, Integrity: Moderate, Availability: Moderate)
requiredFields.azureSource: Azure Policy — compliance state per resource

requiredFields.name: System Boundary
requiredFields.example: All Azure resources within subscription X and VNet Y
requiredFields.azureSource: Azure Resource Graph — subscription and VNet inventory

requiredFields.name: Control Implementation
requiredFields.description: Narrative describing how each control is implemented
requiredFields.example: AC-2: Account management is implemented via Azure AD with conditional access policies
requiredFields.azureSource: Azure Policy compliance results, Microsoft Defender for Cloud recommendations

requiredFields.name: Network Diagram
requiredFields.example: VNet topology with subnet segmentation and NSG rules
requiredFields.azureSource: Azure Network Watcher — topology view and NSG flow logs

azureMappings.Encryption Status: Azure Key Vault and disk encryption status
azureMappings.Vulnerability Scanning: Microsoft Defender for Cloud vulnerability assessments
azureMappings.Logging and Monitoring: Azure Monitor and Log Analytics workspace
azureMappings.Configuration Management: Azure Policy and Azure Automation DSC

authorizationChecklist.description: Complete System Security Plan with all required sections
All applicable controls documented with implementation narratives
Network and boundary diagrams with data flows
All security roles documented with named individuals
ConMon strategy aligned with FedRAMP requirements

templates.title: Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M)
templates.description: Tracks identified vulnerabilities, planned remediation actions, milestones, and completion dates. Required for managing security weaknesses throughout the authorization lifecycle.

sections.name: Weakness Identification
sections.description: Identification and documentation of security weaknesses.

sections.name: Remediation Planning
sections.description: Planned corrective actions and implementation timeline.

sections.name: Status Tracking
sections.description: Current status and progress of remediation efforts.

requiredFields.name: POA&M ID
requiredFields.azureSource: Microsoft Defender for Cloud — finding ID

requiredFields.name: Weakness Description
requiredFields.description: Detailed description of the security weakness
requiredFields.example: TLS 1.0/1.1 enabled on Azure App Service endpoint
requiredFields.azureSource: Microsoft Defender for Cloud — recommendation details

requiredFields.name: Risk Level
requiredFields.example: High — exploitable vulnerability with public exposure
requiredFields.azureSource: Microsoft Defender for Cloud — severity classification

requiredFields.name: Remediation Action
requiredFields.description: Planned corrective action to address the weakness
requiredFields.example: Enforce TLS 1.2 minimum via Azure Policy and App Service configuration

requiredFields.name: Scheduled Completion
requiredFields.azureSource: N/A — manually assigned based on risk and resources

azureMappings.Risk Scoring: Microsoft Defender for Cloud secure score
azureMappings.Remediation Tracking: Azure DevOps work items or ServiceNow integration

authorizationChecklist.description: Complete POA&M with all open findings documented
Each item categorized as High/Moderate/Low risk
Each item has specific milestones with due dates
POA&M updated monthly with current status
Closed items have supporting evidence attached

templates.title: Continuous Monitoring (ConMon) / Customer Responsibility Matrix
templates.description: Defines responsibilities for ongoing security monitoring between CSP and customer. Includes vulnerability scanning schedules, reporting cadence, and incident response procedures.

sections.name: Monitoring Strategy
sections.description: Overall approach to continuous security monitoring.

sections.name: Incident Response
sections.description: Incident detection, response, and reporting integration.

requiredFields.name: Scanning Schedule
requiredFields.example: Weekly OS/infrastructure scans, monthly web application scans
requiredFields.azureSource: Microsoft Defender for Cloud — auto-provisioning and scan scheduling

requiredFields.name: Remediation Timeframes
requiredFields.example: Critical: 30 days, High: 90 days, Moderate: 180 days

requiredFields.name: Reporting Cadence
requiredFields.example: Monthly POA&M updates, monthly scan reports, annual assessment
requiredFields.azureSource: Azure Monitor — scheduled query rules and workbook exports

requiredFields.name: Incident Notification
requiredFields.example: US-CERT notification within 1 hour for significant incidents

azureMappings.Continuous Scanning: Microsoft Defender for Cloud continuous assessment
azureMappings.Alerting: Azure Monitor alert rules and action groups

authorizationChecklist.description: Monthly and annual report templates prepared
CSP vs customer responsibility assignments documented
